title: Browser Extension: DevTools Panel & Sidebar

actors.name: DevTools User
actors.description: Human developer using Chrome DevTools to inspect anti-pattern findings

actors.name: panel.js
actors.description: Main Impeccable DevTools panel UI; communicates via auto-reconnecting port to service-worker

actors.name: sidebar.js
actors.description: Elements sidebar pane; shows findings for currently selected element ($0)

actors.name: devtools.js
actors.description: DevTools page; creates panel + sidebar, manages lifecycle port, optional auto-scan trigger

actors.name: service-worker.js
actors.description: Background service worker; manages panelPorts Map, routes messages to content script

actors.name: Content Script
actors.description: Injected into inspected tab; performs scan, manages overlays/highlights

actors.name: chrome.storage.sync
actors.description: Persists user preferences: disabledRules, lineLengthMode, spotlightBlur, autoScan

use_cases.name: Open DevTools Panel
use_cases.trigger: User opens Chrome DevTools → Impeccable tab
use_cases.details: devtools.js calls chrome.devtools.panels.create('Impeccable', ..., 'devtools/panel.html'). connectLifecycle() opens impeccable-devtools-{tabId} port to SW.

use_cases.name: Auto-Scan on DevTools Open
use_cases.details: devtools.js reads chrome.storage.sync{autoScan}; if 'devtools', posts {action:'scan'} on lifecyclePort. SW calls sendScanToTab(tabId).

use_cases.name: Scan / Rescan Page
use_cases.trigger: User clicks Rescan button in panel
use_cases.details: btnRescan click → showScanning() + postToPort({action:'scan'}). SW receives 'scan' on panel port → sendScanToTab(tabId) → chrome.tabs.sendMessage(tabId, {action:'scan', config}).

use_cases.name: View Anti-Pattern Findings
use_cases.trigger: SW sends {action:'findings'} or {action:'state'} on panel port
use_cases.details: handlePortMessage() calls renderFindings(msg.findings). Findings grouped by category (slop/quality), then by anti-pattern type. Badge count updated.

use_cases.name: Toggle Overlays
use_cases.details: btnToggle click → postToPort({action:'toggle-overlays'}). SW forwards to content script. SW notifies panels with {action:'overlays-toggled'}. updateToggleButton() reflects state.

use_cases.name: Hover Finding to Highlight Element
use_cases.trigger: User moves pointer over finding item in panel list
use_cases.details: container pointermove → setHoveredItem(selector) → postToPort({action:'highlight', selector}). SW forwards to content script. pointerleave/blur → postToPort({action:'unhighlight'}).

use_cases.name: Click Finding to Inspect Element
use_cases.trigger: User clicks a non-page-level, non-hidden finding item
use_cases.details: itemEl click → inspectElement(selector) → chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval('document.querySelector(...).scrollIntoView(); inspect(el)'). Jumps to element in Elements panel.

use_cases.name: Copy Single Finding
use_cases.trigger: User clicks copy icon on a single finding item
use_cases.details: copyBtn click → formatSingleFindingForCopy(item, finding) → getInspectedUrl() via chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval → navigator.clipboard.writeText(). Includes: name, URL, element, detail, description, suggested skills.

use_cases.name: Copy All Findings
use_cases.details: btnCopyAll click → formatFindingsForCopy(currentFindings) → getInspectedUrl() → navigator.clipboard.writeText(). Groups by slop/quality, includes uniqueSkillsForFindings() sorted by frequency.

use_cases.name: Open Settings Panel
use_cases.details: btnSettings click → toggles settingsContainer visibility. Settings show rules grouped by category (AI tells / Quality) with checkboxes.

use_cases.name: Toggle Individual Rule (Enable/Disable)
use_cases.trigger: User checks/unchecks a rule checkbox in settings
use_cases.details: toggleRule(ruleId, enabled) → updates disabledRules array → chrome.storage.sync.set({disabledRules}) → chrome.runtime.sendMessage({action:'disabled-rules-changed'}).

use_cases.name: Configure Line Length Mode
use_cases.trigger: User clicks strict/relaxed button in settings

use_cases.name: View Findings for Selected Element (Sidebar)
use_cases.trigger: User selects an element in the Elements panel
use_cases.details: chrome.devtools.panels.elements.onSelectionChanged → refreshForCurrentSelection() → chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow.eval(match selectors against $0) → render(matchedItems). Sidebar port (impeccable-sidebar-{tabId}) also receives findings updates from SW.

use_cases.name: Auto-Reconnect Port on SW Restart
use_cases.trigger: MV3 service worker terminated after ~30s inactivity
use_cases.details: port.onDisconnect sets port=null. getPort() lazily recreates chrome.runtime.connect. 20s heartbeat interval: postToPort({action:'ping'}) keeps SW alive.

use_cases.name: Re-Scan on Page Navigation
use_cases.trigger: chrome.webNavigation.onCommitted fires for a DevTools-tracked tab
use_cases.details: SW checks devtoolsTabs.has(tabId) && wasActive (had findings or injected). Clears state.findings, sends {action:'navigated'} to panels → showScanning(), then triggers new scan.

use_case_relationships: UC02 <<include>> UC03 (auto-scan is a specialized scan trigger)
UC11 <<include>> UC10 (rule toggle requires settings open)
UC11 <<extend>> UC03 (re-scan may follow rule change)
UC15 <<extend>> UC04 (sidebar shows subset of panel findings)
UC16 <<extend>> UC01 (reconnect happens within panel/sidebar lifecycle)

port_communication.sidebar_port: impeccable-sidebar-{tabId} — added to panelPorts Map; receives findings/state; triggers auto-scan if no findings
port_communication.devtools_lifecycle_port: impeccable-devtools-{tabId} — tracks DevTools open/closed; sends scan (if autoScan=devtools) and ping keepalive
port_communication.panelPorts_map: Map<tabId, Set<port>> in SW — both panel and sidebar ports stored here for broadcast via notifyPanels()

settings_managed: disabledRules: string[] — rule IDs to skip during detection
spotlightBlur: boolean — dim/blur page on hover-highlight
autoScan: 'panel'|'devtools' — when to trigger first scan
important_notes: MV3 service worker lifecycle management is a cross-cutting concern: panel.js, sidebar.js, and devtools.js all implement auto-reconnect + 20s ping heartbeat. initSettings() in panel.js fetches antipatterns.json at runtime and loads chrome.storage.sync on startup. FIX_SKILLS map links each anti-pattern type to suggested Impeccable skills (/distill, /typeset, etc.).
